case_name: KING, SPENCER v. RENTOKIL NORTH AMERICA, INCORPORATED

summary.brief: KING, SPENCER v. RENTOKIL NORTH AMERICA, INCORPORATED. Medical malpractice case involving other.

evidence_index.text: Mr. King injured his right knee in 2023 while working for Rentokil, which accepted the claim.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Mr. King’s authorized doctor, Johnathan Bryant, repaired a meniscus tear in May 2024.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The surgeon also biopsied Mr. King’s knee to see if he was a candidate for a procedure to repair a cartilage defect (ACI).
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Utilization Review physician Eddie Sassoon denied the surgery recommendation.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Bryant appealed to the Bureau’s Medical Director, who overturned the denial and approved the procedure.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The Court holds Rentokil shall authorize the surgery.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: The utilization physician was not licensed to practice in Tennessee, so the utilization review report denying the surgery was invalid.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: The authorized doctor’s recommendation is considered medically necessary under Tennessee Code Annotated section 50-6-204(a)(3)(H) (2024).
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

evidence_index.text: Rentokil has the burden of rebutting this presumption.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

evidence_index.text: The parties submitted records from Dr. Bryant, Dr. Sassoon, and Dr. T. Lisle Whitman, who performed an independent medical examination.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Mr. King injured his right knee in 2023 while working for Rentokil.
initial_state.initial_working_problem: Right knee injury with potential cartilage defect

requestables.name: Orthopedic Surgery Consultation
requestables.description: Consultation with Dr. Johnathan Bryant regarding knee injury

reveal.result_summary: Meniscus tear repaired; biopsy performed for cartilage defect
reveal.decision_impact: Led to recommendation for ACI surgery

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Mr. King presents with a right knee injury

timeline_phases.name: Surgical Workup
timeline_phases.description: Evaluation and recommendation for ACI surgery

decision_points.clinical_context: Decision on surgical intervention for knee injury
decision_points.prompt: Should the ACI surgery be authorized?

options.description: Authorize ACI surgery as recommended by Dr. Bryant
options.clinical_reasoning: Medically necessary as per authorized doctor and Medical Director

options.description: Deny ACI surgery based on utilization review

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant denied ACI surgery based on utilization review

expected_action_court.description: Authorize ACI surgery as per Medical Director's approval
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Medically necessary under Tennessee Code Annotated

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Utilization review physician was not licensed in Tennessee, invalidating the denial
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Surgery should have been authorized based on Medical Director's decision

key_findings: Breach of duty in denying surgery based on invalid utilization review
Court orders Rentokil to authorize ACI surgery

factual_timeline.event: Mr. King injured his right knee

court_findings.finding: Utilization review denial invalid due to unlicensed physician
